comment_text:
Black Mirror basically asks us to escape reality by watching a stylized version of the exact problems crushing us in real life. 
And because it’s an anthology, it never stays long enough to imagine solutions, just long enough to make people feel hopeless. 
Every episode usually ends right when it becomes obvious the characters have zero power to change anything. 

Sure, tragedy is fine; not everything needs a happy ending. 
But in Black Mirror, actual reform, meaningful resistance, or any progress toward fixing the system is almost never on the table.
Just vibes of “reality sucks, you’re doomed, tune in next week for more doom.”

That’s not insight; it’s resignation packaged as prestige TV. Hard pass.

comment_text:
Black Mirror is great of course, but what I'd love to see more of is your smaller shows, that deserve just as much support as your big hitters. That's how you grew this franchise after all.

I, and many many people I know, would be incredibly thrilled with more seasons of Dead Boy Detectives, which gained a respectable 92million hours views in its first month of release alone, and yet was cancelled in August to great surprise, disappointment and anger from the quickly growing fanbase. Niche shows like Dead Boy Detectives, like Black Mirror for example, deserve the chance to fully and explore the detailed elaborate universes they've established in the set up of single season. Please reconsider, or at the very least, release the rights so someone else can take on the challenge. Thousands upon thousands of us campaign daily, as I'm sure you know, since there is a huge billboard up directly outside your LA office right now. We don't know how much clearer we can be!

comment_text:
BM finally made an episode I just gave up on. Could not take Hotel Reverie seriously at all. They use an editing tactic that X Factor and Got Talent auditions are built on - cutting to the audience to spell out how you should feel about something. But the disconnect was too much. No way was I going to buy Rae's eternal startled expression as a compellingly timeless romantic performance. Any real audience (esp. of jaded industry techs) would have been snickering or busy on their workstations instead of staring with their mouths open. Phoney and manipulative and up itself. Made me cringe-quit. 

I would rewatch the Miley Cyrus one instead of finishing this any day of the week.

video_title: Black Mirror: Season 7 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: I just have to say, it barely hit me, but the title of this show is probably the best title for a show ever. For those who don’t know a Black mirror, also known as a scrying is used in witchcraft to see the future. It can be used for evocation, contacting spirits, seeing the past or the present, but mostly seeing the future. And when we turn off our phones, tv’s, computers, etc. we are left with a black screen of our reflection. A literal black mirror. So it’s a brilliant play on words because we are using our black mirrors to see the future (via watching the show). But you can take it a step further if you like conspiracy theories and the occult. Now there’s 2 schools of thought in history and even now there’s a resurgence of witchcraft practitioners. My Grandfather who happened to be born in Mexico and lived there before immigrating when he joined the U.S. military at 18. Where he is from and other parts of Mexico witchcraft is still practiced. Now from what he’s told me and others have told me the first school of thought is there are two types of witchcraft (magic) white magic and black magic. White magic is good. It’s selfless used for healing others spiritually and apparently physically. The intent is good. Then there’s black magic. Which is evil. It’s used to hurt others, or for self gain.  The intent is greed, jealousy, lust, hatred, etc.  Now think about it. What sells on the internet? Sure there is good that goes on, but it’s evil that sells. When you’re on the internet how much negativity do you see compared to the good? I’d wager there’s a lot more bad. Meaning our phones/devices are inherently evil. They are bad for us and we aren’t even aware that our devices have bad magic rooted in them.
